summary.intent: Implement CHA-16: expose additional crm.contacts.* RPC methods (filtered list, getByExternal, getWithChannels) that were implemented in the store layer but not wired to the RPC surface.
summary.outcome: Successfully added 3 new RPC methods (crm.contacts.list with filter params, crm.contacts.getByExternal, crm.contacts.getWithChannels), updated the CrmService trait and LiveCrmService impl, added 12 new tests, and committed all changes. 26 tests pass.

repo: The CRM stack is fully layered: CrmStore trait (21 methods) → CrmService trait → LiveCrmService → RPC registration in services.rs + auth scope in mod.rs. Wiring a new method requires touching all 4 layers.
RPC auth scoping uses READ_METHODS/WRITE_METHODS string slices in crates/gateway/src/methods/mod.rs (lines 90-96, 221-228). New read methods must be added to READ_METHODS.
The project uses nightly rustfmt — format must be applied with nightly toolchain, not stable.
Clippy must be run without --all-features on macOS (no nvcc/CUDA available).
CrmStore has MemoryCrmStore for tests and SqliteCrmStore for prod — service-level tests use the memory impl.
The existing list_contacts() and list_matters() service methods took no params — inconsistent with all other CrmService methods that take params: Value. This had to be fixed to enable filtering.

code.finding: CrmService trait definition — 15 methods. list_contacts() and list_matters() were the only methods without params: Value, requiring a signature change.
All 15 CRM RPC method registrations live here. Each handler follows the pattern: parse params from ctx.params, call service method, serialize result.
13 existing service-level tests using MemoryCrmStore. Two tests (test_list_contacts and one other) called list_contacts() with no args and needed updating when the signature changed.

workflow: The CEO review phase (plan-ceo-review skill) does a nuclear scope challenge and system audit before any implementation — it found that basic CRUD was already done and identified the real gap (3 unwired store methods).
The eng review phase (plan-eng-review skill) produces a formal test plan artifact written to ~/.gstack/projects/... and maps all failure modes before implementation begins.
The pipeline is: ceo-review → eng-review → implement → code-review, each triggered by skill invocations with Linear lifecycle context.
Running a background compile check while reading files saved time during the implement phase.

friction: list_contacts() signature change broke two existing tests that called it with no arguments — required hunting down and fixing callers after the trait change.
Clippy --all-features fails on macOS due to missing nvcc/CUDA — had to know to omit that flag.
The CEO review agent called AskUserQuestion (interactive) in a headless session — violated the ground rule of no user interaction. The session stalled and had to be resumed.
The implement stage prompt had blank Issue/URL/Status fields (template variables not substituted), making it harder to orient to context.
Had to iterate on finding the exact end of the test module in crm_service.rs across multiple Read calls to find the right insertion point.
open_items: crm.channels.getByExternal — store has get_channel_by_external() but it's not wired to RPC; deferred as out-of-scope follow-up ticket.
crm.matters.listFiltered and crm.matters.listByContact — store methods exist but deferred (not in crm.contacts.* namespace).
Unbounded list_contacts with no params still returns all rows — backward-compat was preserved (6B decision) but a default limit for large datasets is a future concern.
Structured CRM error codes — store_err() converts all errors to generic string messages; structured codes (not-found vs constraint-violation vs db-error) would improve client-side handling.
Bulk operations (batch upsert/delete), contact merge/dedup, real-time CRM event subscriptions, fuzzy search — all identified in dream state but out of scope for this PR.
list_matters() still takes no params — same pattern inconsistency as list_contacts() was, noted for follow-up.
